title: TOTO-1 nucleic acid labeling protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ft6","text":"A modified TOTO-1 iodide labeling protocol to enable visualization of nucleic acid loading efficiency, as a means to verify that nucleic acid constructs are entering the cells. With additional cleanup steps to decrease background staining. A convenient assay to use in conjunction with transfection optimization. (Modified from Golzio, M., Teissie, J. \u0026 Rols, M.P. 2002. Direct visualization at the single-cell level of electrically mediated gene delivery. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA 99, 1292–1297.)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Hydrate Microcon-30 kDa centrifugal filter columns at least 1h to overnight with nuclease free sterile water.

Spin columns at 3000xg for 20 minutes, repeat as many times as needed until all water has been pushed through column to collection tube (in older columns, this may require extended spin time. Column must be fully hydrated before proceeding).
On ice, aliquot sufficient volume of DNA or RNA for experiments, want at least 1 ug/uL conc.
Add TOTO-1 dye from stock (1 mg/mL, in DMSO) to desired concentration (see guidelines for dye concentration calculation).
Mix well by gentle pipetting.
Shield from light and incubate on ice for 1 hour
Electroporation steps (follow preferred electroporation protocol)

Allow electroporated cells to incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes in cuvette. This will allow nucleic acid adhered to extracellular wall to enter cellular pores generated during electroporation.

Gently transfer electroporated cells from cuvette to 30 mL falcon tube in addition of 10mL sterile seawater for wash post electroporation. Spin at 3,000xg for no more than 5 minutes to minimize extracellular adhesion loss. The wash step will remove unbound stained nucleic material from the media. Remove supernatant and gently resuspend cell pellet in 0.5mL sterile seawater(adjust final volume based on the desired density of your cell sample) .

Visualize aliquots (drops) 10 min post electroporation in same buffer. Border of cells should show signal on GFP channel but background staining will be high due to residual TOTO-1:DNA complex in solution. ( This is an optional QC step to view staining progress ).
The stained nucleic acid is retained in filter cup of the hydrated spin column in ca 20 μL of TE and will be visible. The eluate and collection tube can be discarded.
Transfer TOTO-1 labeled nucleic acid mixture to Millipore spin filter column.
Resuspend filter retentate in TE buffer, adjust volume added to yield [DNA] ≥ 1 μg/μl (. Gently pipette up and down around the filter to collect all the dye bound dna.
Carrier DNA (e.g Salmon sperm DNA, Thermo-Fisher AM9680 at 1-10ug/mL) may be added to electroporation step to aid in transfection of nucleic material.
a
a
TOTO-1 nucleic acid complex fluorescence overlaps with standard FITC / GFP filter sets (e.g. GFP Light Cube on our Thermo-Fisher EVOS FL inverted microscope).
Add dye bound nucleic material to cells prepared for electroporation (in electroporation buffer). Incubate on ice for 10 minutes.

Filtering transfection samples onto black filters (Isopore HTBP01300, 0.4um pore or equivalent) followed by seawater rinses provides a low noise, high contrast background for TOTO-1 nucleic acid imaging (see below, however, this does not hold true for the Cy5 light cube used to stimulate chlorophyll fluorescence).
Centrifuge for 20 minutes at 3,000xg in microcentrifuge. If filter intact, there should be ca. 20 μL remaining in filter cup.
Add 200 μL TE pH8 to filter cup and centrifuge as in step 8. Repeat if background fluorescence is an issue in procedural blanks.
creator: April L Woods
